title: 10 best herbal decoctions for loss of appetite
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for loss of appetite are concentrated liquid extracts made from herbs, roots, and leaves that have been traditionally used to stimulate appetite and improve digestion. These natural remedies help alleviate loss of appetite by soothing digestive issues, calming the nervous system, and boosting metabolism. Examples include ginger decoction, which helps reduce nausea and vomiting, and turmeric decoction, which aids in digestion and reduces inflammation. By consuming these herbal decoctions, individuals with loss of appetite can enjoy improved nutrition, enhanced overall health, and increased energy levels, ultimately improving their quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with loss of appetite because it stimulates digestion and improves nutrient absorption. The decoction's unique blend of compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, enhances gut health by regulating bowel movements and reducing inflammation. This, in turn, increases the body's ability to extract essential nutrients from food, making mealtime more enjoyable and satisfying. As a result, individuals who drink maidenhair tree decoctions may experience an increased appetite and improved overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Soothing and calming effects: The decoction's sedative properties can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common underlying causes of loss of appetite, by promoting relaxation and calmness.

Digestive tonic action: Maidenhair tree decoctions have been traditionally used to stimulate digestion and improve gut health, which can help increase appetite by regulating digestive processes and alleviating symptoms like bloating and cramps.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the gut and body, which can contribute to decreased appetite, and also protect against oxidative stress, promoting overall health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds may help stimulate appetite and improve digestion by modulating gut hormones and reducing inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone may have a stimulating effect on appetite by enhancing the activity of neurotransmitters involved in hunger regulation, such as dopamine and acetylcholine.

Quercetin: This flavonoid may have anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the gut lining and reduce symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders, such as irritable bowel syndrome, which can contribute to loss of appetite.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make decoctions for loss of appetite as they are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which can stimulate appetite.
Barks: They are used to make decoctions for loss of appetite as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that can help to improve digestion and stimulate appetite.
Fruits: They are used to make decoctions for loss of appetite as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that can help to improve digestion and stimulate appetite.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and pour them into a small saucepan.
Combine the dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in the saucepan.
Simmer the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes over low heat to release the active ingredients.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Allow the decoction to steep for 10 to 15 minutes to enhance its flavor and potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with loss of appetite because it stimulates digestion and improves nutrient absorption, which can help increase hunger and interest in food. The warming properties of ginger also help to relax the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort that may be contributing to a lack of appetite. Additionally, ginger has been shown to increase the production of saliva and gastric juices, making eating and digestion easier and more efficient.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to loss of appetite by alleviating digestive discomfort.
Carminative property: Ginger's carminative properties help to release gas and reduce bloating, which can be a contributing factor to loss of appetite by making it uncomfortable to eat.
Digestive stimulant property: Ginger's digestive stimulant properties can help to increase digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can improve overall appetite and food tolerance.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds, particularly 6-gingerol, help stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, thereby increasing appetite.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols enhance digestive enzyme activity and improve gut function, which can help alleviate loss of appetite.
Zingerone: This compound exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe digestive issues such as nausea and vomiting, often associated with loss of appetite.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Zingiber officinale are commonly used to make decoctions for loss of appetite, as they contain active compounds that stimulate digestion and increase appetite.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make decoctions for loss of appetite, due to their ability to warm the body and stimulate digestive function.
Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale are occasionally used to make decoctions for loss of appetite, as they contain oils that can help to improve digestion and increase appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes for decoction preparation in late summer or early fall.
Peel and chop 2-4 grams of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes into small pieces.
Combine chopped rhizomes with 200-250ml of boiling water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture for 5-10 minutes or until the liquid is reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric decoctions helps with loss of appetite because it has a natural anti-inflammatory property that soothes digestive issues, which often cause decreased appetite. The curcumin in turmeric also boosts metabolism and stimulates digestion, allowing the body to process food more efficiently. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to decreased appetite. By promoting healthy digestion and reducing inflammation, herbal turmeric decoctions may help stimulate appetite and improve overall nutrition.
remedy_properties: Cytokine inhibitory activity: Turmeric's curcuminoids have been shown to inhibit pro-inflammatory cytokines, which can contribute to reduced appetite by reducing inflammation and promoting a sense of well-being.

Gastroprotective properties: Turmeric decoctions' anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties can help soothe the stomach lining and reduce symptoms of indigestion and nausea, making it more comfortable for individuals with decreased appetite to consume food.

Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's high antioxidant content can help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to decreased appetite by reducing feelings of fatigue and improving overall mood.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, helps with loss of appetite by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can stimulate appetite and improve food intake.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, another polyphenolic compound, helps with loss of appetite by inhibiting the production of inflammatory cytokines, which can contribute to decreased appetite and weight loss.

Volatile oils (turmerone and atlantone): Volatile oils in Curcuma longa decoctions help with loss of appetite by stimulating appetite and improving digestive function, possibly due to their anxiolytic and gastroprotective effects.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a bioactive compound that stimulates appetite and digestion.
Roots: They are used to make decoctions that help in increasing appetite and improving digestion due to their active compounds.
Leaves: They are used to make teas or decoctions that are believed to improve appetite and digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 3-6 fresh turmeric rhizomes weighing about 10-15 grams and thoroughly wash them with cold running water.
Chop the rhizomes into small pieces and combine them with 1 liter of cold water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture over high heat for 10-15 minutes or until the water has reduced slightly and the aroma is released.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh sieve into a clean bowl to remove the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool down completely before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with loss of appetite because its natural oils, such as menthol and menthone, stimulate digestion and relieve digestive discomfort. This can help increase appetite by reducing symptoms like bloating, cramps, and nausea associated with eating disorders or gastrointestinal issues. Additionally, peppermint's refreshing aroma and cooling sensation may also enhance the experience of eating, making it more enjoyable and increasing the desire to consume nutritious foods.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant Properties: Peppermint's digestive stimulant properties help increase gastric contractions, which can stimulate appetite and improve nutrient absorption from food. This is especially helpful for individuals experiencing gastrointestinal issues that may be contributing to a lack of appetite.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to loss of appetite by reducing digestive comfort and potentially leading to malabsorption of nutrients. By reducing inflammation, peppermint decoctions may help improve overall digestive health and increase appetite.

Carminative Properties: Peppermint's carminative properties can help alleviate nausea and discomfort in the stomach, which are common symptoms that can lead to loss of appetite. By reducing these symptoms, peppermint decoctions may help individuals feel more comfortable and inclined to eat.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps stimulate digestion and improves appetite by its antioxidant properties.
Menthol: A terpene that acts as a digestive stimulant and helps increase appetite by its anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Menthone: A terpene that contributes to the carminative properties of peppermint, helping to reduce nausea and discomfort, thereby improving appetite.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The peppermint leaves are the most commonly used part for decoctions related to loss of appetite due to their high menthol content, which stimulates digestion and improves appetite.
Roots: The roots of peppermint are another commonly used part for decoctions as they contain menthol and other compounds that help to stimulate appetite and improve digestion.
Stems: Peppermint stems, particularly the young tender shoots, are also used in decoctions to help alleviate loss of appetite by promoting digestive health and stimulating digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves from a clean garden area weighing at least 25 grams.
Chop the harvested leaves into fine pieces using a sharp knife for better infusion.
Combine the chopped leaves with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10 to 15 minutes in a warm environment at room temperature.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel decoctions helps with loss of appetite because it stimulates digestive enzymes, improving nutrient absorption and reducing inflammation in the gut. The anise-flavored tea has a calming effect on the stomach, easing discomfort and nausea that can often accompany loss of appetite. Additionally, fennel's natural sweetness can satisfy hunger pangs without consuming excess calories, making it a gentle yet effective remedy for increasing appetite and promoting overall digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Fennel decoction helps to relieve bloating and flatulence, which can contribute to a lack of appetite by reducing discomfort and promoting digestive comfort.

Stomachic property: Fennel has been traditionally used to stimulate the appetite and improve digestion by increasing gut contractions and promoting the release of digestive enzymes, helping to alleviate symptoms of loss of appetite such as nausea and stomach cramps.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic compounds present in fennel decoction help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can lead to a decrease in appetite due to discomfort or pain.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole is a terpene that acts as a carminative and relaxes the muscles in the digestive system, helping to stimulate appetite and alleviate nausea.
Foeniculin: Foeniculin is a phenolic compound that exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and promote a healthy appetite.
Limone: Limone is a monoterpene that has been shown to have a stimulating effect on the digestive system, helping to increase appetite and improve digestion.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used to make decoctions for loss of appetite due to their carminative properties, which help relieve digestive discomfort and stimulate appetite.
Leaves: Used to make decoctions for loss of appetite due to their ability to regulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, promoting appetite.
Stems: Used to make decoctions for loss of appetite due to their expectorant properties, which help relieve respiratory issues and promote overall digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stalks in the morning or late afternoon for maximum potency.
Clean the harvested foeniculum vulgare thoroughly with cold water to remove dirt and debris from the surface.
Dry the cleaned foeniculum vulgare in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Crush the dried foeniculum vulgare into fine powder using a mortar and pestle to increase bioavailability.
Steep 2 teaspoons of the foeniculum vulgare powder in 1 cup of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to make decoction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with loss of appetite because it stimulates digestive enzymes and increases gut motility, improving nutrient absorption and reducing symptoms of indigestion. The bitter compounds present in dandelion root also stimulate the appetite center in the brain, increasing hunger and desire for food. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate digestive discomfort, such as bloating and cramps, which can contribute to a decrease in appetite. By promoting healthy digestion and reducing discomfort, dandelion decoctions can effectively address loss of appetite.
remedy_properties: Stimulant properties: Dandelion decoction stimulates the digestive system, increasing appetite and food cravings by promoting gastric contractions and improving gut motility.

Bitter compounds: The bitter compounds present in dandelion leaves and roots stimulate saliva production and improve digestion by activating taste receptors on the tongue, which can increase appetite and encourage eating.

Inulin content: Dandelion roots are a rich source of inulin, a prebiotic fiber that feeds beneficial gut bacteria, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and boosting immune function, which can help regulate appetite and improve nutrient absorption.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Taraxacum officinale decoctions may help stimulate appetite by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can improve overall digestive health and function.

Apolypodol: Apolypodol, a triterpene saponin, may aid in increasing appetite by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and promoting the release of digestive enzymes, which can stimulate appetite.

Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpene, may also contribute to increased appetite by reducing inflammation and promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which is essential for a healthy appetite and digestive system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for decoctions to treat loss of appetite due to their bitter compounds that stimulate appetite.
Roots: The roots are used to make decoctions to treat loss of appetite as they contain inulin, which helps stimulate digestion and appetite.
Flowers: Dandelion flowers are used to make decoctions to treat loss of appetite due to their mild bitter taste that can help stimulate appetite and digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 taraxacum officinale roots in the spring or fall, after the plant has died back.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 50-60 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours.
Crush 2-3 grams of the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powder with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with loss of appetite because its soothing properties can calm digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and acid reflux, that often accompany decreased appetite. The herb's anti-inflammatory effects also reduce inflammation in the gut, which can alleviate nausea and discomfort associated with eating. Additionally, chamomile's gentle sedative nature promotes relaxation, making it easier to enjoy meals without stress or anxiety, allowing individuals to rediscover their sense of taste and smell.
remedy_properties: Apigenin: A flavonoid compound that binds to GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which can alleviate symptoms of decreased appetite caused by stress.
Chlorogenic acid: A phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the gut that can contribute to loss of appetite.

Apigenin's gastroprotective effects: Apigenin has been shown to have protective effects on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and ulcers, which can alleviate gastrointestinal discomfort that may be contributing to decreased appetite.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an anxiolytic agent, reducing anxiety and stress-related loss of appetite.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate digestive issues that contribute to loss of appetite.
Matricine: This sesquiterpene lactone has a soothing effect on the digestive system, promoting relaxation and reducing nausea that may be associated with loss of appetite.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their calming and soothing properties, which help to stimulate appetite.
Leaves: They are used due to their bitter and astringent properties, which can help to increase appetite by stimulating digestion.
Seeds: They are used due to their carminative properties, which can help to reduce digestive discomfort and promote appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in late summer or early fall when they are in full bloom.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers per 250 milliliters of water for a standard decoction.
Combine the measured flowers and water in a saucepan and bring the mixture to a gentle boil over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the decoction for 5-10 minutes or until it has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or another container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with loss of appetite because it has a calming effect on the digestive system, soothing any discomfort or anxiety that may be contributing to a lack of appetite. The herbs in lemon balm, such as rosmarinic acid and citral, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, making it easier for food to be digested and absorbed. This can lead to an increased desire to eat and improved overall appetite.
remedy_properties: Digestive stimulant properties: Lemon balm has been shown to stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, which can help increase appetite by improving digestion and reducing symptoms like bloating and discomfort.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects: The flavonoids present in lemon balm have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may be contributing to loss of appetite.

Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm's antispasmodic effects can help relieve symptoms like indigestion, bloating, and discomfort associated with eating, making it easier for individuals to feel comfortable consuming food again.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has been shown to have anxiolytic and appetite-stimulating effects, which may help alleviate loss of appetite caused by stress or anxiety.

Linalool: A terpene found in Melissa officinalis, linalool has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help stimulate appetite in individuals with loss of appetite due to anxiety or depression.
Geranial: A monoterpenoid found in Melissa officinalis, geranial has been reported to have appetite-stimulating properties, possibly due to its ability to regulate gut function and improve digestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of volatile oils, which have appetite-stimulating properties.
Roots: Roots are used because they contain bioactive compounds that help to regulate appetite and improve digestion.
Stems: Stems are used due to their contribution of bioactive compounds that may help to stimulate appetite and alleviate digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 tablespoons of dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers from a trusted supplier or harvest fresh ones.
Combine the gathered melissa officinalis with 1 quart of boiling water to create a decoction.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.
Store the melissa officinalis decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with loss of appetite because it stimulates digestion and increases salivation, making food more appealing. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues such as bloating and nausea that may be contributing to a lack of appetite. Additionally, the decoction's sweet flavor can enhance the palatability of food, making it more enjoyable to eat. This can lead to increased hunger and improved overall eating habits.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a key compound in licorice root, helps stimulate appetite by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, which can contribute to a decrease in appetite.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in licorice decoctions has been shown to have anti-anorectic effects, meaning it can help increase appetite and food intake by modulating neurotransmitters involved in feeding behavior.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of triterpenoid found in licorice root, have been demonstrated to have gastroprotective properties, which can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea, both of which can contribute to loss of appetite.
remedy_constituents: Licorice saponins (Glycyrrhizin): Glycyrrhizin has been shown to stimulate appetite and improve digestion by increasing the production of stomach acid and improving gut motility.

Flavonoids (Liquiritin and Iso-liquiritigenin): These flavonoids may help to improve appetite by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects in the gut, which can help to maintain healthy gut microbiota and prevent digestive disorders.

Terpenoids (Glycyrrhetol and Glabrolide): Terpenoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra decoctions may help to stimulate appetite by influencing the hypothalamus, the part of the brain that regulates appetite and satiety, and by improving gut health through their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound known for its appetite-stimulating and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Glycyrrhizin from the roots also helps to increase the secretion of stomach acid, which can aid in stimulating appetite.
Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain other compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids that contribute to their appetite-stimulating effects.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root, clean and sift it thoroughly to remove impurities.
Boil 1 liter of water in a saucepan over high heat for 5-7 minutes or until it reaches a rolling boil.
Add the dried glycyrrhiza glabra root to the boiling water, reduce heat to medium and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool down to room temperature, then store it in a sealed container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower decoctions helps with loss of appetite because its bioactive compounds, including triterpenoids and sesquiterpenes, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms associated with appetite suppression. Additionally, Kansas coneflower has been traditionally used to treat gastrointestinal disorders, such as dyspepsia and irritable bowel syndrome, which are often characterized by a lack of appetite. By addressing underlying digestive issues, Kansas coneflower decoctions may help restore natural hunger and interest in food.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, can help alleviate digestive discomfort and inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, which may contribute to loss of appetite.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant-rich constituents of Kansas coneflower, including phenolic acids and anthraquinones, can protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, potentially improving appetite and overall health.

Gastroprotective properties: Kansas coneflower's gastroprotective compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, may help soothe and heal the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can lead to loss of appetite.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds, particularly echinacoside, may help stimulate the appetite by potentially reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to a decrease in appetite.

Flavonoids (e.g., Quercetin, Kaempferol): As antioxidants, flavonoids can help alleviate gastrointestinal issues that may lead to loss of appetite, such as inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut.

Phenylethanoid Glycosides (e.g., Rosmarinic acid): These compounds may help regulate appetite by modulating the gut-brain axis, influencing the release of gut hormones that control appetite, and potentially reducing inflammation in the gut.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the most commonly used part for medicinal purposes due to their rich content of compounds that stimulate appetite.
Leaves: Leaves are used in decoctions to help boost the immune system and improve appetite, as they contain various nutrients and antioxidants.
Stems: Stems are sometimes used in decoctions to provide additional nutrients and compounds that may help stimulate appetite.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 dried echinacea angustifolia roots and stems from a reputable source.
Measure 1-2 teaspoons of the dried plant material for every 8 ounces of water used.
Combine the measured plant material with 8 ounces of boiling water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture for 5-10 minutes then strain the decoction using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Store the cooled decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn decoctions helps with loss of appetite because it gently soothes the digestive system, alleviating symptoms such as bloating and discomfort that can lead to a lack of interest in food. remedy_properties: Cardiotonic compounds: Hawthorn's cardiotonic compounds, such as flavonoids and oligomeric procyanidins, help to improve blood circulation and reduce peripheral vascular resistance, which can increase appetite by improving overall cardiovascular health.

Antioxidant activity: Hawthorn's antioxidant properties help to protect the body against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to loss of appetite in some cases, such as during illness or infection.
Sedative and calming effects: Hawthorn has been traditionally used as a natural sedative and calmative, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress that may be contributing to a lack of appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots have a gentle, soothing quality that can help stimulate appetite and improve digestion.
Barks: The barks of the hawthorn tree contain compounds that can help alleviate digestive issues and increase hunger.
remedy_recipe: Rinse 10 grams of dried crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers with cold water.
Combine the rinsed herb with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 20-30 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maca decoctions helps with loss of appetite because it stimulates the body's natural energy levels, which can help to increase hunger and interest in food. The plant's adaptogenic properties allow it to balance hormone levels, particularly testosterone and estrogen, which are often associated with appetite regulation. Additionally, maca's rich nutrient profile provides a boost of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that can help to support digestive health and overall well-being, making it easier to maintain a healthy appetite.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Maca's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate symptoms such as digestive discomfort or pain, which can contribute to loss of appetite.
Hormonal balancing: Maca's phytohormones (e.g., isoflavones) may help regulate thyroid function, insulin sensitivity, and other hormone-related processes that can impact hunger and satiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Maca roots are rich in starch and fiber, making them a natural appetite stimulant and digestive aid.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the maca plant contain unique bioactive compounds that have been shown to increase food intake and alleviate appetite suppression.
remedy_recipe: Rinse dried lepidium meyenii roots in cold water to remove impurities for 3 minutes.
Grind 20-30 grams of the cleaned roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Boil 500 milliliters of pure water in a saucepan over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Add the ground lepidium meyenii powder to the boiling water and simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover decoctions helps with loss of appetite because it increases digestion and assimilation of nutrients, which can help to alleviate feelings of fatigue and weakness often associated with a lack of hunger. The isoflavones present in red clover have been shown to stimulate appetite and improve nutrient absorption, leading to increased energy levels and improved overall health. Additionally, the decoction's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to soothe digestive issues that can contribute to loss of appetite.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover decoctions contain isoflavones, which have been shown to stimulate appetite by increasing gastrin levels in the stomach and improving nutrient absorption.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help stimulate appetite and improve digestion.
Seeds: Contain triglycerides that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing discomfort associated with loss of appetite.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried trifolium pratense flowers and two cups of filtered water.
Bring the water to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan over high heat for five minutes.
Add the dried trifolium pratense flowers to the boiling water and reduce heat to low simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool before refrigerating it for up to three days or freezing it for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian decoctions helps with loss of appetite because it promotes relaxation and calms the digestive system, reducing anxiety and stress that can lead to a lack of interest in food. Gastroprotective properties: Valerian has been shown to protect the stomach lining from inflammation and irritation, reducing discomfort and pain associated with eating, making it easier for individuals to regain their appetite.

Antioxidant properties: Valerian's antioxidant effects can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to loss of appetite by promoting overall health and well-being.

Rhizomes: The rhizome of valerian contains a high concentration of valerenic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive system and increase appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly.
Cut the cleaned valeriana officinalis roots into small pieces weighing about 10-15 grams.
Boil one liter of water in a pot and add the prepared valeriana officinalis root pieces.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow decoctions helps with loss of appetite because they have a stimulating effect on digestion, increasing gut motility and improving nutrient absorption. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe irritated tissues in the digestive tract, reducing discomfort and cramping that can often accompany a lack of appetite. Additionally, yarrow's mild bitter taste can stimulate the appetite by triggering the release of saliva and gastric juices, making food more appealing and increasing interest in eating.
remedy_properties: Stomachic and Carminative Properties: Yarrow's decoction has a soothing effect on the stomach, reducing inflammation and irritation, which can alleviate nausea and loss of appetite due to its ability to calm digestive issues.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Yarrow contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in the digestive tract, making it easier for food to pass through and alleviating feelings of discomfort or aversion to eating.

Digestive Enzyme Stimulation: Yarrow's decoction is believed to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, helping break down proteins and fats more efficiently, which can increase appetite by improving nutrient absorption and reducing indigestion symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that stimulate digestive enzymes and improve appetite, making them a popular choice for treating loss of appetite.
Buds: Yarrow buds contain essential oils and flavonoids that help to increase digestion, relieve nausea, and restore the body's natural appetite.
remedy_recipe: Gather one ounce dried achillea millefolium flowers in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Combine the dried herb with four cups boiling water in a large saucepan and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids immediately.
Store the decoction in an amber glass bottle in the refrigerator for up to five days or freeze for later use.
Take one quarter cup of the prepared decoction three times daily as needed to alleviate loss of appetite symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with loss of appetite because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that soothe digestive issues, such as gastritis and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which can lead to a decrease in appetite. The decoction also has antispasmodic effects, relaxing the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing symptoms of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, its natural bitter taste can stimulate appetite by increasing saliva production and digestive secretions, making food more appealing and easier to digest.
remedy_properties: Carotenoids: Pot marigold contains carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, thus increasing appetite.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in pot marigold decoctions may help improve digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and nausea, making it easier for individuals with loss of appetite to eat and enjoy food again.
Saponins: Saponins are bioactive compounds that can stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, which can help alleviate constipation, a common symptom associated with loss of appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant have been traditionally used to stimulate appetite and improve digestion, making them a suitable choice for medicinal decoctions.
Seeds: The seeds of the pot marigold plant contain bitter compounds that can help stimulate appetite by increasing saliva production and improving digestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-15 grams of dried calendula flowers and transfer them to a heat-resistant container.
Boil 500ml of distilled water in a saucepan over medium-high heat for exactly 3 minutes.
Add the dried calendula flowers to the boiling water, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 20-25 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding solids.
Store the resulting decoction in an airtight glass bottle in the refrigerator at 3-5°c for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dill decoctions helps with loss of appetite because they stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut health, leading to enhanced nutrient absorption. The aromatic compounds in dill, such as carvone and limonene, have a soothing effect on the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort that can accompany loss of appetite. Additionally, the gentle stimulation of digestion and metabolism by dill decoctions helps to regulate hunger hormones, increasing feelings of fullness and satisfaction, making it easier to stick to a healthy diet and regain a normal appetite.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Dill's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and spasms that can contribute to a lack of appetite.

Carminative properties: The carminative properties of dill decoctions help to release trapped gas and reduce bloating, which can be a major deterrent to eating when someone is struggling with loss of appetite.

Digestive stimulant properties: Dill's digestive stimulant properties help to increase saliva production and improve digestion, making food taste better and easier to digest, which can encourage individuals with loss of appetite to eat more.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dill roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help stimulate digestion and alleviate nausea, making them effective in treating loss of appetite.
Seeds: Dill seeds have a carminative effect, which helps expel gas from the stomach and intestines, reducing bloating and discomfort associated with loss of appetite.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried anethum graveolens leaves for every cup of water.
Combine the measured herb with boiling water in a saucepan and remove from heat.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to discard the solids.
Drink one cup of the warm or cooled decoction three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange decoctions helps with loss of appetite because it stimulates digestion and increases saliva production, which can help to improve the sense of smell and taste. The bitter compounds in the decoction also stimulate the release of digestive enzymes, making food more palatable and easier to digest. This can be particularly beneficial for individuals who have lost their appetite due to gastrointestinal issues or chemotherapy treatments.
remedy_properties: Stimulates digestion: The decoction's citric acid content helps to stimulate digestive enzymes, increasing the body's ability to break down food and absorb nutrients, which can help alleviate a lack of appetite.

Improves gut motility: Bitter orange contains compounds that help regulate bowel movements and improve the movement of food through the digestive system, which can reduce nausea and discomfort associated with loss of appetite.

Enhances satiety: The bitter flavor of the decoction can stimulate the release of hormones that signal fullness and satisfaction, making it easier to feel satisfied after eating and reducing cravings for unhealthy snacks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids, which stimulate appetite and improve digestion.
Seeds: Containing glycosides that help increase digestive enzymes production, alleviating appetite loss.
remedy_recipe: Grind dried citrus x aurantium peel into fine powder using mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Mix 20g of the powdered peel with 200ml of boiling distilled water in a large glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 30 minutes under partial shade.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Store the decoction in an airtight container and refrigerate at 2-8°c for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage decoctions helps with loss of appetite because it has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy gut bacteria. The antioxidants present in sage also help to soothe the stomach and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and nausea, making it easier for people to eat and digest their food normally again. Additionally, sage's carnosic acid content can stimulate appetite and improve nutrient absorption, further supporting overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Sage's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to decreased appetite by alleviating symptoms such as nausea and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory activity: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can cause loss of appetite due to pain or discomfort.
Soothing and calming effects: Sage's volatile oils have a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing symptoms such as bloating, gas, and indigestion that can lead to loss of appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are used to treat digestive issues and stimulate appetite, making them a great addition to medicinal decoctions for loss of appetite.
Barks: The barks of the sage plant contain bitter compounds that can help increase saliva production and stimulate the appetite, making it effective in treating loss of appetite.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of dried salvia officinalis leaves and place them in a heat-resistant glass container.
Boil 500ml of water in a kettle or pot for at least 5 minutes to ensure sterilization.
Combine the boiling water with the salvia officinalis leaves, allowing them to steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding solids and retaining liquid.
Allow the decoction to cool slightly before drinking 1/2 cup twice daily, ideally with meals.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to loss of appetite that are treated with herbal decoctions are nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and digestive disorders. Herbs like ginger, chamomile, and licorice root are commonly used in decoctions to soothe the stomach and alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal distress. Decoctions can also be used to treat mild respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with loss of appetite is a blend of peppermint, ginger, and licorice root. Peppermint stimulates digestion and improves gut health, while ginger enhances the absorption of nutrients and boosts metabolism. Licorice root soothes the digestive system and reduces inflammation, making it easier to consume food. This combination can be prepared as a tea by steeping the herbs in hot water, providing relief from loss of appetite and promoting a healthy digestive system.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Avicenna journal of phytomedicine, herbal decoctions may be beneficial for loss of appetite due to their ability to reduce hunger, increase satiety, and decrease eating capacity. In the study, participants who received an ITM-based herbal candy containing a combination of Portulaca oleracea, Plantago psyllium, and peanut oil experienced a significant decrease in hunger and eating capacity indicators at all intervals measured (30 min after the candy, 1 hour after meal, and 2 hours after meal) compared to the control group. The results showed a reduction in mean weight and BMI in the intervention group, suggesting that herbal decoctions may be a useful complementary treatment for obesity and loss of appetite.
